text.title: Elicitation Techniques Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the elicitation-techniques methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (plan): Pick techniques per source class; schedule with consent + PII policy.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the plan gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (collect consent and pii rules): Sign consent; pre-tag PII categories; brief stakeholders.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the collect consent and pii rules gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (run sessions): Execute each technique per its template; capture typed artifact.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the run sessions gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (triangulate): Synthesise REQ stubs citing ≥2 distinct technique sessions.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the triangulate gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (commit): Commit to version control; link from requirements documentation.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the commit gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for elicitation-techniques do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
